title: 4 Establishing Policies for ATM and POS Transactions
body_text:
4	Establishing Policies for ATM and POS Transactions
4.1	Policy on Complying with PCMLTFA
Establish a policy that complies with
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
on training staff who process ATM transactions. Refer to the Financial Transactions and Reports Analysis Centre of Canada (FINTRAC) for more information.
4.2	Policy on Investigating Unauthorized/Fraudulent Transactions
Establish a policy on training staff to handle unauthorized ATM or POS transactions. See the applicable operating rules for acquirer and issuer responsibilities regarding response deadlines, cheque verification, and exception items.
4.3	Policy for Statutory Declarations
Establish a policy on the requirement of statutory declarations. Central 1芒聙聶s statutory declaration forms, available on the Client Centre, must be sworn in front of a commissioner (e.g., lawyer). The Statutory Declaration 芒聙聯 Unauthorized Account Transactions, Form 3757, is used specifically for unauthorized ATM or POS transactions. This form is only available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program.
Your financial institution can choose to accept statutory declarations sworn in other provinces; but there is an associated risk. The statutory declaration may have been sworn validly for the province the commissioner is working in but doesn芒聙聶t meet the requirements in BC or Ontario. If you choose to accept a statutory declaration sworn in another province, we recommend you consider the reason the declaration was signed in the other province and if it芒聙聶s reasonable for your customer and/or circumstances.
We recommend that you require a case file number from the RCMP (or a local police department) for its statutory declaration. The unauthorized transaction(s) might be related to a larger fraudulent scheme that the police department is investigating. Filing a case with the police department also reduces the risk of fraudulent claims being made.
Refer to the completion instructions for the statutory declaration for more information (only available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program).
Related Information
Statutory Declaration 芒聙聯 Unauthorized Account Transactions,
Form 3757
Completion Instructions for Statutory Declaration 芒聙聯 Unauthorized Account Transactions, Form 3757
END
